content.problemStatement:
A competition with NNN participants (numbered 111 through NNN) is taking place in Chefland. There are N−1N-1N−1 rounds in the competition; in each round, two arbitrarily chosen contestants battle, one of them loses and drops out of the competition.

There are 101010 types of weapons (numbered 111 through 101010). You are given NNN strings S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​; for each valid iii and jjj, the jjj-th character of SiS_iSi​ is '1' if the iii-th contestant initially has a weapon of type jjj or '0' otherwise. During each battle, for each type jjj such that both contestants in this battle currently have weapons of type jjj, these weapons of both contestants are destroyed; after the battle, the winner collects all remaining (not destroyed) weapons of the loser. Note that each contestant may win or lose regardless of the weapons he/she has.

Chef is feeling bored watching the contest, so he wants to find the maximum possible number of weapons the winner of the tournament could have after the last battle, regardless of which contestants fight in which battles or the results of the battles. Can you help him?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single string SiS_iSi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum number of weapons the winner could have.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: If the first person defeats the second person, weapons 111, 333, 777 and 101010 are destroyed. Then, if the third person defeats the first person (who now has weapons 222, 555, 888 and 999), weapons 999 are destroyed and the winner has weapons 222, 555, 888 and 101010.
